PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS, CALENDAR OF DEC-16-2005 THE COURT FINDS THAT THE SURVIVORS TRUST PROVIDES BARBARA KERAN BLUMENFELD WITH A SPECIFIC INTEREST OF AN 11.333% INTEREST IN KERAN FRIEDMAN LLC AND PROVIDES SUSAN KERAN SOLOMON WITH A SPECIFIC INTEREST OF A 5.333%. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT THE ALLOCATION OF SAID INTERET IS DETERMINED AT THE TIME OF THE DECEDENTS DEATH. calendar.matters:
Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-01-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Theodore Kolb appearing for Barbara Blumenfeld. All briefs are in. Mr. Kolb argued that he does not agree with the examiner's notes. Matter submitted.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-10-2007 Continued To Jan-24-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky (Counsel for Susan Solomon, Co-Trustee); Theodore Kolb (Counsel for Barbara Blumenfeld, Co-Trustee).

The Court initially queried counsel as to whether the Court retained jurisdiction in light of the pending appeal and if so, whether a bond on appeal was required pursuant to Probate Code Section 1310. Counsel also addressed the issue as to how the bond would be funded in light of the nature of the assets held by the Trust. Given the recent advent of this issue, the Court requested that Mr. Skootsky file a brief on the issues on or before January 17, 2007 with Mr. Kolb to file a response on or before January 24, 2007, whereupon the Court would take the matter under submission.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722 ; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Tim Kneis.

Motion To Confirm The Referees Report And Recommendation
Off Calendar Nov-09-2006 Text Ruling

Motion To Confirm The Referees Report And Recommendation
Off Calendar Sep-01-2006 Continued To Sep-15-06 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Theodore Kolb and Liman Bradford appearing for and with moving party Barbara Blumenfeld. Seth Skootsky and Constance Yu appearing for and with Susan Keran Solomon, respondent co-trustee. After listening to oral argument from both sides. It appears to the Court that it was the decedent's intend to have his daughters share equally. It would have to be 50-50 from any interest received after the date of death. Court instructs Mr. Kolb to prepare an order consistent with the Court's preliminary ruling before and Mr. Skootsky to prepare an order consistent with today's tentative ruling. Matter will stand submitted when the Court gets the orders. Per Diem fees paid by both sides. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Teena Paxton CSR#1558. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Motion To Confirm The Referees Report And Recommendation
Off Calendar Aug-16-2006 Continued To Sep-01-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Motion To Confirm The Referees Report And Recommendation
Off Calendar Aug-10-2006 Continued To Aug-18-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Theodore Kolb appearing for Barbara Blumenfeld. Seth Skootsky appearing for Susan Solomon. Court wants to know the difference between the two orders. Issues discussed. Both counsel agree to referral to Judge Hanlon to resolve the two issues. It will be up to Judge Hanlon to determine the extension of testimony he wants. Mr. Skootsky to prepare order and he will send it to Mr. Kolb for approval as to form. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
